hindi_meaning: अब योग का अनुशासन प्रारम्भ होता है।
english_meaning: Now begins the authoritative instruction on yoga.
context: The word 'atha' (now) implies that the student has prepared themselves — through life experience, suffering, and readiness. Yoga begins only when one is truly ready.
practical_application: You picked up this practice not by accident. The restlessness, the longing for change — that readiness brought you here. The 'atha' moment has arrived.

hindi_meaning: योग चित्त की वृत्तियों का निरोध है।
english_meaning: Yoga is the cessation of the fluctuations (movements) of consciousness.
context: This is the definition of yoga. The mind (chitta) constantly moves — vrittis are thoughts, memories, fantasies, fears. Yoga is the stilling of all these movements.
practical_application: Every time you sit in meditation and watch your thoughts without following them, you are practicing yoga. Every time you resist an urge by staying in the witness state, you are practicing yoga.

hindi_meaning: तब द्रष्टा अपने स्वरूप में स्थित हो जाता है।
english_meaning: Then the seer abides in its own true nature.
context: When the chitta-vrittis are silenced, the pure seer — the Atman — shines in its own light. This is the goal: to know oneself as the witness, not the witnessed.
practical_application: In moments of mental silence — during pranayama, deep meditation, or even after intense exercise — you touch this state. The peace you feel in those moments is your true nature.

hindi_meaning: अन्य समय में द्रष्टा की वृत्तियों से तादात्म्य हो जाता है।
english_meaning: At other times, the seer identifies with the fluctuations of consciousness.
context: Most of the time, we mistake ourselves for our thoughts and emotions. When a lustful thought arises and we think 'I am lustful,' we have identified with the vritti. The practice is to dis-identify.
practical_application: When an urge arises, you are not the urge. You are the witness of the urge. Say: 'There is an urge appearing in my consciousness.' That small shift — from 'I want' to 'there is wanting' — is yoga.

hindi_meaning: अभ्यास और वैराग्य से चित्तवृत्तियों का निरोध होता है।
english_meaning: The control of those fluctuations is achieved through sustained practice (abhyasa) and dispassion (vairagya).
context: Patanjali gives the two-winged formula: you need both practice AND dispassion. Practice alone without detachment leads to spiritual materialism. Detachment without practice leads to passivity.
practical_application: Abhyasa: show up every day — for your sadhana, your morning practice, your cold shower. Vairagya: don't be attached to how 'good' the practice feels. Both wings are needed to fly.

sanskrit: स तु दीर्घकाल नैरन्तर्य सत्कारासेवितो दृढभूमिः।
transliteration: sa tu dirgha-kala nairantarya satkara-asevito dridha-bhumih
hindi_meaning: वह अभ्यास जो दीर्घकाल तक, निरंतर, और श्रद्धा के साथ किया जाए, वह दृढ़ भूमि बन जाता है।
english_meaning: That practice becomes firmly grounded when cultivated for a long time, without interruption, and with sincere devotion.
context: Three conditions make practice powerful: dirgha-kala (long time), nairantarya (unbroken continuity), and satkara (with respect, earnestness, devotion). Even a brief daily practice done every single day beats an intense practice done sporadically.
practical_application: Your 60-day sadhana is dirgha-kala. Not missing a single day is nairantarya. Doing it with sincerity — not just going through the motions — is satkara. All three together build an unshakeable foundation.

hindi_meaning: सुखियों के साथ मैत्री, दुखियों के साथ करुणा, पुण्यात्माओं के साथ प्रसन्नता, और पापियों के साथ उपेक्षा — इन भावनाओं से चित्त प्रसन्न होता है।
english_meaning: The mind becomes serene by cultivating friendship toward the happy, compassion toward the suffering, joy toward the virtuous, and equanimity toward the wicked.
context: Patanjali's 'four locks and four keys' — a practical formula for social harmony and inner peace. Most of our mental suffering comes from wrong emotional reactions to others.
practical_application: When you see someone who seems more 'advanced' or happier, feel maitri (genuine joy for them, no jealousy). When someone is struggling, feel karuna. When someone is virtuous, feel mudita (celebrate it). When someone is toxic, feel upeksha (neutral distance).

hindi_meaning: तप, स्वाध्याय और ईश्वरप्रणिधान — ये तीन क्रियायोग हैं।
english_meaning: Tapas (austerity/self-discipline), Svadhyaya (self-study/scripture study), and Ishvara Pranidhana (surrender to God) — these three constitute Kriya Yoga (yoga in action).
context: This is the practical trinity of spiritual life. Tapas purifies the body and mind. Svadhyaya illuminates the self. Ishvara Pranidhana surrenders the ego. Together, they are the complete practice for the householder and student.
practical_application: Tapas: your brahmacharya, your cold shower, your early rising. Svadhyaya: your scripture reading, your journaling. Ishvara Pranidhana: your morning prayer, your surrender of results. You are already practicing Kriya Yoga.

hindi_meaning: अविद्या, अस्मिता, राग, द्वेष और अभिनिवेश — ये पाँच क्लेश (दुःख के कारण) हैं।
english_meaning: The five afflictions are: avidya (ignorance), asmita (ego-identification), raga (attachment), dvesha (aversion), and abhinivesha (clinging to life/fear of death).
context: All human suffering can be traced to these five roots. Raga — attraction/craving — is the root of addictive behavior. The practice is to see these as movements in consciousness, not as 'me'.
practical_application: Sexual urge is raga. The habit loop is raga feeding itself. Avidya (thinking pleasure will satisfy you) makes raga possible. Svadhyaya (self-study) destroys avidya, which weakens raga. Each scripture you read strikes at the root.

hindi_meaning: यम, नियम, आसन, प्राणायाम, प्रत्याहार, धारणा, ध्यान और समाधि — ये योग के आठ अंग हैं।
english_meaning: The eight limbs of yoga are: yama (ethical restraints), niyama (observances), asana (posture), pranayama (breath control), pratyahara (sense withdrawal), dharana (concentration), dhyana (meditation), and samadhi (absorption).
context: Patanjali's Ashtanga (eight-limb) yoga is the complete map of spiritual practice. It begins with ethics (yama/niyama) because without ethical foundation, higher practices can cause harm or ego-inflation.
practical_application: Brahmacharya is one of the five yamas (2.30). Your practice is literally the first limb of classical yoga. You are not just doing a challenge — you are walking an ancient path.

hindi_meaning: अहिंसा, सत्य, अस्तेय, ब्रह्मचर्य और अपरिग्रह — ये पाँच यम हैं।
english_meaning: The five yamas (ethical restraints) are: non-violence (ahimsa), truthfulness (satya), non-stealing (asteya), continence/brahmacharya, and non-possessiveness (aparigraha).
context: Brahmacharya is explicitly listed as the fourth of the five great ethical vows (mahavrata) of yoga. It is not a modern invention or a religious rule — it is the very foundation of classical yoga philosophy.
practical_application: You are not following some random internet challenge. You are practicing a mahavrata — a great vow — listed by one of history's greatest philosophers. This vow is 2,500+ years old. Millions of yogis have walked this path before you.

hindi_meaning: शौच, संतोष, तप, स्वाध्याय और ईश्वरप्रणिधान — ये पाँच नियम हैं।
english_meaning: The five niyamas (personal observances) are: shaucha (purity), santosha (contentment), tapas (austerity), svadhyaya (self-study), and Ishvara pranidhana (surrender to God).
context: The niyamas are internal disciplines — personal vows between you and your practice. Shaucha includes physical cleanliness (your skincare, hygiene) and mental purity (sattva). Tapas is your brahmacharya itself.
practical_application: Your daily log tracks shaucha (skincare morning/evening), tapas (brahmacharya), svadhyaya (shloka learned), and ishvara pranidhana (prayers). You are living all five niyamas daily.

hindi_meaning: ब्रह्मचर्य के प्रतिष्ठित होने पर वीर्यलाभ (महान शक्ति की प्राप्ति) होती है।
english_meaning: When brahmacharya is firmly established, tremendous vitality (virya) is gained.
context: This is the most directly relevant sutra to your practice. Patanjali says when brahmacharya becomes pratishthita — firmly established, not just practiced occasionally — virya is gained. Virya is not just sexual energy but the fundamental life-force that powers intelligence, strength, spiritual insight, and charisma.
practical_application: The energy you feel building — the sharpness of mind, the confidence in your eyes, the steadiness in your voice — this is virya. Patanjali documented it 2,500 years ago. You are experiencing an ancient, verified transformation.

hindi_meaning: शौच (पवित्रता) से अपने शरीर के प्रति वैराग्य और दूसरों के साथ असंसर्ग (विवेकपूर्ण दूरी) होती है।
english_meaning: From shaucha (purity) comes a certain disenchantment with one's own body and an avoidance of unnecessary contact with others.
context: As you cultivate inner purity through brahmacharya, you naturally become less obsessed with the body — both your own and others'. This is not self-hate but liberation from body-identification.
practical_application: When brahmacharya is established, the constant mental chatter about physical attraction naturally quiets. Not because you suppress it — because you've outgrown it. This is a natural sign of progress.

hindi_meaning: इससे सत्त्व की शुद्धि, मन की प्रसन्नता, एकाग्रता, इन्द्रियजय और आत्मदर्शन की योग्यता प्राप्त होती है।
english_meaning: From purity also comes purification of sattva, cheerfulness of mind, one-pointedness, control of the senses, and fitness for Self-realization.
context: These five benefits arrive in sequence as purity deepens. First the mind becomes clear (sattva-shuddhi), then cheerful (saumanasya), then focused (ekagrya), then the senses come under control (indriya-jaya), and finally the Self can be known (atma-darshana).
practical_application: You may have noticed this sequence: first clearer thinking, then better mood, then longer meditation, then reduced urges. This is not imagination — it is Patanjali's documented map of inner transformation.

hindi_meaning: संतोष से अनुत्तम (सर्वोत्तम) सुख की प्राप्ति होती है।
english_meaning: From contentment comes unsurpassed happiness.
context: Contentment (santosha) is not passive resignation. It is the active choice to be at peace with what is — while still striving for improvement. It is the opposite of craving.
practical_application: The happiness from sensory gratification always has an end — and a comedown. The happiness from santosha has no ceiling and no comedown. Every day you practice contentment instead of craving, you accumulate a deeper happiness.

hindi_meaning: तप से अशुद्धि का नाश होने पर शरीर और इन्द्रियों में सिद्धि प्राप्त होती है।
english_meaning: From tapas, on the destruction of impurities, comes perfection of body and senses.
context: Tapas — austerity, disciplined practice — burns away impurities in the body and mind (like fire purifies gold). As impurities reduce, both body and senses become sharper, healthier, and more refined.
practical_application: Your discipline (tapas) is literally purifying you — your skin clears, your eyes brighten, your mind sharpens. Patanjali confirms: kaya-siddhi (bodily perfection) is a fruit of tapas. You are experiencing this.

hindi_meaning: स्वाध्याय से इष्टदेवता का साक्षात्कार होता है।
english_meaning: From svadhyaya (self-study/scripture study) comes contact with one's chosen deity (ishta devata).
context: Svadhyaya has two meanings: study of the self through introspection, and study of sacred texts. As you study the Gita, the Yoga Sutras, the Upanishads — and as you journal your own inner states — your ishta devata becomes more real and alive.
practical_application: Every shloka you learn, every scripture verse you contemplate, every journal entry you write — is svadhyaya. Patanjali says this practice brings you into actual contact with the Divine. Your daily scripture is a portal, not just reading.

hindi_meaning: ईश्वरप्रणिधान से समाधि की सिद्धि होती है।
english_meaning: From surrender to God (Ishvara pranidhana) comes the attainment of samadhi.
context: The ego is the final obstacle to samadhi. Complete surrender dissolves the ego. Ishvara pranidhana is not weakness — it is the supreme act of strength: releasing the illusion of control.
practical_application: Your morning prayer is not ritual — it is Ishvara pranidhana. When you say 'I surrender the results to You,' you are practicing the very niyama that leads to samadhi. Small surrenders accumulate into liberation.

hindi_meaning: आसन स्थिर और सुखद होना चाहिए।
english_meaning: Asana (posture) should be stable and comfortable.
context: For Patanjali, asana is not the gymnastic forms of modern yoga — it is the ability to sit still, steadily, comfortably for extended meditation. The goal is a body that does not distract the mind.
practical_application: When you sit for pranayama or meditation, comfort and steadiness are the standard — not performance. A simple cross-legged seat, spine tall, body relaxed, for 20 minutes — that is perfect asana.

hindi_meaning: इन्द्रियों का अपने विषयों से संपर्क न होकर चित्त के स्वरूप का अनुकरण करना प्रत्याहार है।
english_meaning: Pratyahara is when the senses withdraw from their objects and follow the form of consciousness itself.
context: Pratyahara — the fifth limb — is sense withdrawal. The senses, instead of reaching outward toward objects, turn inward and follow the direction of consciousness. It is the bridge between the outer limbs (yama, niyama, asana, pranayama) and the inner limbs (dharana, dhyana, samadhi).
practical_application: This is what you practice when you close your eyes and focus on breath instead of giving attention to a triggering image or sensation. The senses can be redirected. That redirection — right when the urge peaks — is pratyahara. It is the fifth limb of yoga, practiced in real time.

hindi_meaning: इससे इन्द्रियों पर परम वश प्राप्त होती है।
english_meaning: From pratyahara comes the supreme mastery over the senses.
context: The result of pratyahara practice is complete indriya-vashyata — mastery of the senses. Not suppression, but mastery. The senses work for you, not against you.
practical_application: Right now you may feel like the senses are in charge and you are their servant. With consistent pratyahara practice, this reverses. The senses gradually come under your direction. This is the promise of sutra 2.55.

hindi_meaning: चित्त को किसी एक स्थान पर बाँधना धारणा है।
english_meaning: Dharana (concentration) is the binding of consciousness to a single place/object.
context: After sense withdrawal, the mind can focus. Dharana is choosing an object — a mantra, a flame, the breath, an image of the deity — and holding consciousness there. The first moment of concentration.
practical_application: When you count your mantra, or watch the flame of the diya, or follow the breath during pranayama — you are practicing dharana. The practice of holding attention is the muscle that grows through your daily sadhana.

hindi_meaning: उसी स्थान पर प्रत्यय (चित्त की वृत्ति) की निरंतर एकतानता ध्यान है।
english_meaning: When the continuous flow of cognition toward that object becomes unbroken, that is dhyana (meditation).
context: Dharana is holding attention. Dhyana is when that holding becomes a continuous, unbroken flow — like oil poured from one vessel to another. The effort of holding disappears; the attention flows naturally.
practical_application: You may have had moments in meditation where you forgot you were meditating — you just were. That state where the watcher and the watched seem to merge — even for a few seconds — is the beginning of dhyana.

hindi_meaning: जब ध्यान में केवल ध्येय वस्तु ही चमकती है और ध्याता का स्वरूप शून्य हो जाता है — वह समाधि है।
english_meaning: When the object alone shines forth in meditation, as if devoid of the form of the meditator — that is samadhi.
context: Samadhi is the pinnacle — when the meditator, the act of meditation, and the object of meditation merge into one. The sense of 'I am meditating' dissolves. Only the object remains, luminously.
practical_application: You don't need to reach samadhi to benefit from yoga. The direction matters more than the destination right now. But knowing where the path leads — this gives your daily practice a sacred context.

hindi_meaning: अथवा विशोका (दुःखरहित) और ज्योतिर्मय (प्रकाशयुक्त) चित्तवृत्ति से भी मन शांत होता है।
english_meaning: Or by focusing on a luminous, sorrowless inner light, the mind becomes stable.
context: Patanjali offers this as a practical technique for stabilizing the mind: visualize a luminous, grief-free inner light at the heart center. This is the jyoti meditation of Indian tradition.
practical_application: When the mind is agitated, try this: close your eyes, visualize a small flame of pure light in your heart. It is without sorrow, without anxiety. Just pure, peaceful light. Breathe into it. This is an ancient anti-anxiety technique.

hindi_meaning: अन्य (साधारण) साधकों के लिए श्रद्धा, वीर्य, स्मृति, समाधि और प्रज्ञा — इनसे समाधि प्राप्त होती है।
english_meaning: For others (non-born-yogis), samadhi is preceded by: faith (shraddha), energy (virya), memory/mindfulness (smriti), meditative absorption (samadhi), and discrimination (prajna).
context: The five steps to samadhi for the earnest aspirant. Shraddha (faith) comes first — you must believe the path leads somewhere. Virya (energy/vitality) — which brahmacharya builds — is second. Then mindfulness, meditation, wisdom.
practical_application: Virya — built through brahmacharya — is literally the second step on the path to samadhi according to Patanjali. Your practice is not separate from your spiritual aspiration. It IS the spiritual path.

hindi_meaning: अहिंसा के प्रतिष्ठित होने पर उस साधक के सामने सब वैर छोड़ देते हैं।
english_meaning: When ahimsa (non-violence) is firmly established, in the presence of that being, all enmity is abandoned.
context: Notice the parallel structure with sutra 2.38: 'brahmacharya-pratishthayam virya-labhah.' When any yama is firmly established, a corresponding power manifests. Ahimsa brings peace to surroundings; brahmacharya brings vitality.
practical_application: As brahmacharya establishes, you will notice a new quality of energy around you — people respond differently. You become calmer, your presence more settled. This is virya — not just physical energy but a field effect.
